title: Bun SQLite file DB: strict + create open options
description: Align file-backed SQLite construction with Bun bun:sqlite documented semantics by passing both strict: true and create: true when opening the app dev file database path so a cold start can create the DB file after the parent directory is ensured. Keep mkdirSync(dirname(databasePath), { recursive: true }) in with-todo-sqlite-repository.ts before repository creation. Limit in-memory Database usage to tests; update direct Database(...) calls in tests only if required for consistent file-create behavior. Do not add staging or production persistence work or expand README/env scope beyond what this change strictly requires.
acceptance: openDatabaseAndMigrate (src/infrastructure/sqlite/open-and-migrate.ts) opens the file-backed database with { strict: true, create: true } per Bun sqlite docs for the configured file path.
with-todo-sqlite-repository.ts still calls mkdirSync(dirname(databasePath), { recursive: true }) before creating the repository / opening the DB.
Cold dev path: after parent mkdir, first open creates the SQLite file and migrations complete without DatabaseOpenError when the file did not exist.
In-memory SQLite remains limited to tests (or other explicitly justified cases); any direct new Database(...) in tests is updated only if needed for consistency with file open semantics.
No new staging or production persistence requirements, env vars, or documentation scope are added as part of this task.
Repository layering unchanged: no react/next imports under domain/application; persistence stays in infrastructure implementing application ports per architecture brief.
